eligibility: Candidate must be an Indian National and should have passed HSC with Physics and Mathematics along with one subject like Chemistry, Biology, or Computer Science. Minimum 45% marks (40% for reserved categories). Must appear in MHT-CET or JEE.
documents: Required documents include MHT-CET scorecard, SSC and HSC marksheets, school leaving certificate, domicile certificate, Aadhaar card, passport photos. Additional documents include caste certificate, income certificate, EWS certificate, and gap certificate if applicable.
reservation: Reservation includes 13% SC, 7% ST, 19% OBC, total 50%. Additional quotas: Defence 5%, PWD 5%, EWS 10%, Female 30%.
branches: Popular engineering branches include Computer Engineering, Information Technology, Mechanical Engineering, Electronics and Telecommunication. Computer and IT are most in demand.
exam: MHT-CET exam includes Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics. There is no negative marking. Mathematics has higher weightage.
admission: Admission is done through CAP (Centralized Admission Process) including registration, document verification, and seat allotment.
colleges: Top colleges include COEP Pune, VJTI Mumbai, SPIT Mumbai. Good percentile for top colleges is above 95.
preparation: Preparation tips include solving previous papers, revising formulas, and studying Maharashtra board books.
